documentation.value: Displays a line of text to standard output.
Options: `-n` suppress trailing newline, `-e` enable backslash escapes, `-E` disable backslash escapes
The string(s) to display
Reads a line from standard input and splits it into fields, assigning to variables.
Display prompt before reading input
Do not treat backslash as an escape character
Read into an indexed array variable
Variable name(s) to assign the input fields to
Formats and prints data according to a format string, similar to C printf.
A format string with specifiers like %s, %d, %f
Arguments to substitute into the format string
Evaluate a conditional expression. Returns 0 (true) or 1 (false).
The conditional expression, e.g. `-f file`, `-d dir`, `str1 = str2`, `n1 -eq n2`
Sets environment variables so they are available to child processes.
Variable name and optional value to export
Remove the export property from the named variable
Reads and executes commands from the given file in the current shell environment.
The file to source
Positional parameters passed to the sourced script
Defines actions to be taken when the shell receives a signal.
A command string to execute when the signal is received, or `-` to reset
Signal name or number (e.g. SIGINT, EXIT, ERR, SIGTERM)
Sets or unsets shell options and positional parameters.
Exit immediately if a command exits with a non-zero status
Treat unset variables as an error during parameter expansion
Print commands and their arguments as they are executed
Set a named option like `pipefail`, `nounset`, `errexit`
Shifts positional parameters to the left by n positions (default 1).
The number of positions to shift (default is 1)
Parses positional parameters as options. Used in while loops for option processing.
The string of recognized option letters; a colon after a letter means it takes an argument
Variable name to store the current option letter
Strips the directory and optionally the suffix from a file path.
The file path
An optional suffix to remove from the result
Strips the last component from a file path, returning the directory portion.
The file path

Outputs the given strings to standard output.
```bash
echo "Hello World"
echo -n "No newline"
echo -e "Line1\nLine2"
```
Do not output the trailing newline.
Enable interpretation of backslash escapes (`\n`, `\t`, etc.).
One or more strings to output.

Reads a line of input and splits it into variables.
```bash
read -p "Enter name: " name
read -r line < file.txt
read -t 5 -p "Quick! " answer
```
Do not treat backslashes as escape characters.
Display `prompt` before reading input (without trailing newline).
Time out and return failure after `timeout` seconds.
Use `delim` as the line delimiter instead of newline.
Variable name(s) to assign input to. Defaults to `REPLY` if omitted.

Evaluates a conditional expression and returns 0 (true) or 1 (false).
```bash
test -f /etc/passwd && echo "File exists"
[ -d /tmp ] && echo "Directory exists"
[ "$a" = "$b" ] && echo "Equal"
```
A conditional expression: `-f file`, `-d dir`, `-z string`, `-n string`, `s1 = s2`, `n1 -eq n2`, etc.

Declares variables and sets attributes.
```bash
declare -i num=42
declare -a arr=(1 2 3)
declare -A map=([key1]=val1 [key2]=val2)
declare -r CONST="immutable"
```
Declare an indexed array variable.
Declare an associative array variable.
Give the variable the integer attribute.
Make the variable readonly.
Mark the variable for export to child processes.
Variable name with optional initial value.

Creates a local variable within a function.
```bash
my_func() {
  local name="Alice"
  local -i count=0
  echo "$name"
}
```
Variable name with optional initial value, scoped to the current function.

Marks variables for export to child processes.
```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"
export MY_VAR="hello"
export -n MY_VAR  # unexport
```
Remove the export property from the variable.
Variable name with optional value to export.

Defines actions to execute when the shell receives a signal.
```bash
trap 'echo Interrupted; exit' INT TERM
trap 'rm -f /tmp/lockfile' EXIT
trap '' SIGINT  # ignore SIGINT
```
Command string to execute when signal is received. Use `''` to ignore, `-` to reset.
One or more signal names or numbers: `EXIT`, `INT`, `TERM`, `HUP`, `ERR`, `DEBUG`, etc.

Sets or unsets shell options and positional parameters.
```bash
set -euo pipefail
set -x  # enable debug tracing
set +x  # disable debug tracing
```
Exit immediately if a command exits with non-zero status.
Treat unset variables as an error.
Print each command before executing (debug mode).
Set option by name: `pipefail`, `noclobber`, `nounset`, etc.
Outputs the given strings to standard output, separated by spaces and followed by a newline.
Suppress the trailing newline
Enable interpretation of backslash escape sequences
The string(s) to output
Prints arguments to standard output. More portable than echo for zsh scripts.
Ignore escape conventions of echo
Suppress the trailing newline
Output to file descriptor fd instead of stdout
Use printf-style format string
The string(s) to print
Reads a line from standard input and assigns words to the named variables.
Raw mode — do not treat backslash as escape character
Read exactly num characters
Timeout in seconds
Variable name(s) to assign input to
Declares variables with specific attributes such as type, scope, and formatting.
Declare as integer variable
Declare as indexed array
Declare as associative array (hash)
Declare as read-only
The variable name and optional initial value
Marks a function for autoloading from a file in `$fpath`.
Suppress alias expansion during loading
Use zsh-style function definition
The name of the function to autoload
Defines style settings used by completion and other zsh subsystems. Patterns match contexts.
A context pattern, e.g. `:completion:*:*:*`
The style name, e.g. `menu`, `list-colors`, `matcher-list`
The value(s) for the style
Old-style completion control (legacy). Defines completion behavior for specific commands.
Complete from the elements of the named array
Call function to generate completions
The command(s) this completion applies to
Adds completion matches from within a completion function.
Words are names of arrays containing the completions
A prefix to prepend to each completion
A suffix to append to each completion
The completion strings to add
Loads or unloads zsh modules (e.g. `zsh/zpty`, `zsh/regex`, `zsh/stat`).
Autoload: mark module to load on first use
Unload the specified module
The module name, e.g. `zsh/complist`, `zsh/datetime`
Binds a key sequence to a widget (command) in the specified or current keymap.
Select the emacs keymap
Select the vi keymap
The key sequence, e.g. `'^R'`, `'^[[A'`, `'\e.'`
The ZLE widget to bind to, e.g. `history-incremental-search-backward`
Enables one or more shell options.
The option to enable, e.g. `AUTO_CD`, `EXTENDED_GLOB`, `CORRECT`, `HIST_IGNORE_DUPS`, `NO_BEEP`
Disables one or more shell options.
The option to disable
